question.text: Under Regulation 7 of SAST, what is the minimum open offer size that an acquirer must make to shareholders of the target company?

answer.text: 20% of total shares of the target company
15% of total shares of the target company
10% of total shares of the target company
26% of total shares of the target company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 7 of SAST mandates a minimum open offer of 26% of total shares of the target company, calculated as on the 10th working day from closure of the tendering period. **The Trap:** 20% is a common distractor because it was the threshold under the old Takeover Code. The revised SAST regulations increased this to 26%. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) extensively tests SAST thresholds. NISM Series VIII also touches on takeover-related corporate actions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 26% open offer is a floor, not a ceiling — acquirers can offer for more shares, but never less. Always verify the exact percentage when advising clients on tendering decisions.
